While both caffeine and narcotics can affect the central nervous system, they do so in very different ways. Narcotics, also known as opioids, are substances that act on opioid receptors in the brain to reduce pain perception and produce feelings of pleasure. Common examples include morphine, heroin, and synthetic opioids like fentanyl.  On the other hand, caffeine is a stimulant that primarily affects adenosine receptors in the brain. ALM involves a range of processes and tools designed to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of software development and maintenance. This encompasses various stages such as planning, requirements gathering, design, implementation, testing, deployment, and ongoing maintenance.
